GSM742944.title: human_liver.single
GSM742944.study_title: Widespread regulated alternative splicing of single codons accelerates proteome evolution
GSM742944.study_summary: Thousands of human genes contain introns ending in NAGNAG motifs (N any nucleotide), where both NAGs can function as 3' splice sites, yielding isoforms differing by inclusion/exclusion of just three bases. However, the functional importance of NAGNAG alternative splicing is highly controversial. Using very deep RNA-Seq data from sixteen human and eight mouse tissues, we found that approximately ha
GSM742944.llm_rationale: Sample is plain 'liver' (human_liver.single) from a multi-tissue splicing reference study.

GSM2326089.study_summary: S-adenosyl methionine (SAM) is a ubiquitous methyl donor that was reported to have chemo- protective activity against liver cancer, however the molecular footprint of SAM has been unknown. GSM2326089.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'normal primary liver cells' with titles 'NorHep.ctrl/treatment' described as 'primary liver cells, not the cell lines'.

GSM2055788.title: Liver_rep2
GSM2055788.study_title: RNA Sequencing Facilitates Quantitative Analysis of Transcriptomes in Human Normal and Cancerous Tissues
GSM2055788.study_summary: Circular RNAs (circRNAs) represent a novel class of widespread and diverse endogenous RNAs that may regulate gene expression in eukaryotes. However, the regulation and function of human circRNAs remain largely unknown. Here we generate ribosomal-depleted RNA sequencing data from six normal tissues and seven cancers, and detect at least 27,000 circRNA candidates.
GSM2055788.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver' from a 'normal tissue panel for circRNA discovery'.

GSM2142335.summary: 17 week humn fetal liver sorted form haematopoietic stem/progenitor cells
GSM2142335.study_title: Differentiation of human embryonic stem cells to HOXA+ hemogenic vasculature that resembles the aorta-gonad-mesonephros
GSM2142335.study_summary: The production of definitive haematopoietic stem/progenitor cells from human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) remains a significant challenge. GSM2142335.llm_rationale: Sample is '17 week human fetal liver sorted for haematopoietic stem/progenitor cells' (FL_HSPC), i.e. blood progenitors, not liver parenchyma.

GSM1695909.title: human lv 1
GSM1695909.study_title: Analysis of human, chimpanzee, macaque and mouse tissue transcriptomes using Next Generation Sequencing
GSM1695909.study_summary: We performed deep strand-specific sequencing of poly-adenylated RNA (polyA+ RNAseq) from human, chimpanzee, macaque and mouse tissues, with the goal of detecting numerous non-annotated poorly expressed and antisense genes. We identified thousands of annotated and novel genes, especially in testis. We discovered that ~2% of the human and chimpanzee multiexonic genes were specific from such species.
GSM1695909.llm_rationale: Sample titles 'human lv 1/2' from a 'cross-species tissue panel including normal human liver'.

GSM1554468.title: Fetal_liver_RNA_seq_rep2
GSM1554468.study_title: Epigenomic landscape during organ formation in human early embryos
GSM1554468.study_summary: Here we present the whole genome ChIP-Seq analyses of a wide variety of histone marks, H3K27ac, H3K4me1, H3K4me3, and H3K27me3 in the brain, heart, and liver, along with the RNA-seq data of these organs of early human embryos 12 weeks after gestation.
GSM1554468.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'fetal liver' with titles 'Fetal_liver_RNA_seq' from '12-week embryonic liver tissue'.

GSM1417183.title: patient2_metastasis
GSM1417183.study_title: Transcriptomic profiling of sequential tumours from breast cancer patients provides a global view of metastatic expression changes following endocrine therapy
GSM1417183.study_summary: We profiled primary breast cancer, nodal and liver metastatic tumours from three patients. At the time of initial diagnosis, all three patients presented with luminal breast cancer with adjacent nodal metastasis. They all received 5 years of enodrine therapy and all subsequently developed liver metastasis.
GSM1417183.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver Metastasis Tumour' from 'primary breast cancer patients who developed liver metastasis'.

GSM1622386.title: Diseased Liver 1
GSM1622386.study_title: Expression of gallstone disease-associated genes in non-diseased and diseased gallbladder and liver samples
GSM1622386.study_summary: Following a large-scale genome-wide association study of gallstone disease, we performed RNA sequencing from tissues of four human gallbladders (3 healthy controls and 1 case with chronic gallstones) and one liver sample from the gallstone case. We aimed to determine the expression patterns of gallstone disease-associated genes in gallbladder and liver, two organs of interest in disease etiology.
GSM1622386.llm_rationale: Sample is 'Liver, adult, diseased' from a study of 'gallstone disease-associated genes' in liver from a chronic gallstones patient.

GSM2199518.title: L_1061
GSM2199518.study_title: Next Generation Sequencing of liver and subcutaneous fat tissues obtained from obese subjects
GSM2199518.study_summary: Patients had low calorie diet weight reduction run in prior to the day of surgery. The human liver and subcutaneous fat tissue samples were obtained from 12 obese  subjects undergoing bariatric surgery and then used for the mRNA expression analyses.
GSM2199518.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Liver tissue of obese patient' with titles 'L_1008'..'L_1045' from morbidly obese bariatric surgery patients.

GSM1548454.title: ICC9 (RNA-seq)
GSM1548454.study_title: Massive parallel sequencing uncovers actionable FGFR2-PPHLN1 fusion and ARAF mutations in intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma
GSM1548454.study_summary: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (iCCA) is a fatal bile duct cancer with dismal prognosis and limited therapeutic options. By performing RNA- and exome sequencing analyses we have discovered a novel fusion event, FGFR2-PPHLN1 (16%), and damaging mutations in the ARAF oncogene (11%).
GSM1548454.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'iCCA patient, liver' with titles like 'ICC10 (RNA-seq)' indicates intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma tumor tissue.

GSM1707675.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'fetal hepatocytes isolated from fetal liver' used as primary fetal hepatocyte reference.

GSM2113310.title: V3-61
GSM2113310.study_title: Impact of RNA degradation on fusion detection by RNA-seq
GSM2113310.study_summary: Purpose: Popular methods for library preparation in RNA-seq such as Illumina TruSeq® RNA v2 kit use a poly-A pulldown strategy. Such methods can cause loss of coverage at the 5’ end of genes, impacting the ability to detect fusions when used on degraded samples. GSM2113310.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver' described as 'liver tissue used in an RNA degradation methods study' with no disease indication.

GSM1548459.study_summary: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (iCCA) is a fatal bile duct cancer with dismal prognosis and limited therapeutic options. By performing RNA- and exome sequencing analyses we have discovered a novel fusion event, FGFR2-PPHLN1 (16%), and damaging mutations in the ARAF oncogene (11%).
GSM1548459.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'iCCA patient, liver' with titles like 'ICC10 (RNA-seq)' indicates intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma tumor tissue.

GSM1662561.title: Blood_Platelets_Chol-376
GSM1662561.study_title: RNA-seq of tumor-educated platelets enables blood-based pan-cancer, multiclass and molecular pathway cancer diagnostics
GSM1662561.study_summary: We report RNA-sequencing data of 283 blood platelet samples, including 228 tumor-educated platelet (TEP) samples collected from patients with six different malignant tumors (non-small cell lung cancer, colorectal cancer, pancreatic cancer, glioblastoma, breast cancer and hepatobiliary carcinomas). In addition, we report RNA-sequencing data of blood platelets isolated from 55 healthy individuals. T
GSM1662561.llm_rationale: Sample title is 'Blood_Platelets_Chol-376' — blood platelets, not liver tissue, despite the cholangiocarcinoma context.

GSM2199516.title: L_1055
GSM2199516.study_title: Next Generation Sequencing of liver and subcutaneous fat tissues obtained from obese subjects
GSM2199516.study_summary: Patients had low calorie diet weight reduction run in prior to the day of surgery. The human liver and subcutaneous fat tissue samples were obtained from 12 obese  subjects undergoing bariatric surgery and then used for the mRNA expression analyses.
GSM2199516.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Liver tissue of obese patient' with titles 'L_1008'..'L_1045' from morbidly obese bariatric surgery patients.

GSM2113305.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver' described as 'liver tissue used in an RNA degradation methods study' with no disease indication.

GSM1417182.title: patient1_metastasis
GSM1417182.study_title: Transcriptomic profiling of sequential tumours from breast cancer patients provides a global view of metastatic expression changes following endocrine therapy
GSM1417182.study_summary: We profiled primary breast cancer, nodal and liver metastatic tumours from three patients. At the time of initial diagnosis, all three patients presented with luminal breast cancer with adjacent nodal metastasis. They all received 5 years of enodrine therapy and all subsequently developed liver metastasis.
GSM1417182.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver Metastasis Tumour' from 'primary breast cancer patients who developed liver metastasis'.

GSM1505571.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Liver' described as 'normal liver' in the CaptureSeq tissue panel.

GSM2170953.title: lb3_G1
GSM2170953.study_title: Multilineage communication regulates human liver bud self-organization from pluripotency I
GSM2170953.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2170953.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver bud' with titles 'lb1_A1' etc. matches iPSC-derived liver bud differentiation.

GSM2170955.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2170955.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver bud' with titles 'lb1_A1' etc. matches iPSC-derived liver bud differentiation.

GSM2170882.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2170882.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver bud' with titles 'lb1_A1' etc. matches iPSC-derived liver bud differentiation.

GSM2170866.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2170866.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver bud' with titles 'lb1_A1' etc. matches iPSC-derived liver bud differentiation.

GSM2232217.title: C173
GSM2232217.study_title: Transcriptional response to hepatitis C virus infection and interferon alpha treatment in the human liver
GSM2232217.study_summary: Hepatitis C virus (HCV) is widely used to investigate host-virus interactions and cellular responses to infection have been extensively studied in vitro. In human liver, interferon (IFN) stimulated gene expression can mask direct transcriptional responses to virus infection. To better characterize the direct effects of HCV infection in vivo, we analyze the transcriptomes of HCV-infected patients l
GSM2232217.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver biopsy' from study of 'liver biopsies from HCV-infected patients' makes these HCV-infected liver tissue.

GSM2388499.title: 785_NC_S2
GSM2388499.study_title: Loss of EGFR-DDEF1 signaling in metastatic and unresectable hepatoblastoma
GSM2388499.study_summary: We report that the application of PE sequencing of mRNA in hepatoblastoma patient biopsies effectively discriminates genes that are expressed, poised for expression, or stably repressed, and therefore reflect cell state and lineage potential. Lysine 36 trimethylation marks primary coding and non-coding transcripts, facilitating gene annotation. Trimethylation of lysine 9 and lysine 20 is detected
GSM2388499.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Normal liver' with 'NC' (normal control) titles from a hepatoblastoma study, most consistent with matched non-tumor liver from cancer patients.

GSM2453421.title: polyA mRNA RNA-seq from liver (ENCLB828MEI)
GSM2453421.summary: Homo sapiens liver tissue male adult (18 years)
GSM2453421.study_summary: RNA-seq of human liver tissue. The second technical replicate was performed in May 2015 and was not part of the original Lin et al. 2014 publication (PMID:25413365).  For data usage terms and conditions, please refer to http://www.genome.gov/27528022 and http://www.genome.gov/Pages/Research/ENCODE/ENCODE_Data_Use_Policy_for_External_Users_03-07-14.pdf
GSM2453421.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Homo sapiens liver tissue male adult (18 years)' from ENCODE with no disease indication.

GSM2610521.title: iPS-LPC 1
GSM2610521.study_title: An in vitro human liver model by iPSC-derived parenchymal and non-parenchymal cells
GSM2610521.study_summary: We have established culture systems to generate liver progenitor cells (LPCs), liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs), and hepatic stellate cells (HSCs) from hiPSCs. Then, we established co-culture system of hiPSC-derived liver cells and performed RNA-seq of hiPSC-derived liver model.
GSM2610521.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'hiPSC-derived liver progenitor cells' with titles 'iPS-LPC' generated from hiPSCs.

GSM2753380.title: PHH 1d
GSM2753380.study_title: Gene expression patterns define the hepatocyte-like cells derived by different strategies [RNA-seq]
GSM2753380.study_summary: We performed genome-wide gene profiling in hiPSC-Hep and hiHep and compared gene expression patterns in these two types of hepatocyte-like cells.Genes involving in fat digestion and absorption and metabolism enzymes were induced in both hiHeps and hiPSC-Heps. There were also hepatic genes not expressed in either hiPSC-Heps or hiHeps, including cytochrome P450-based metabolism genes and coagulation
GSM2753380.llm_rationale: sample titles 'PHH 0d | PHH 1d | PHH 2d | PHH 4d' are Primary Human Hepatocytes cultured over a time course as reference.

GSM2809205.title: Liver_ALGS3
GSM2809205.study_title: RNA Seq of Alagille liver biopsies
GSM2809205.study_summary: Needle biopsies were performed to obtain liver samples from patients for clinical purposes from patients with Alagille syndrome. A small portion was snap frozen and later used for RNA sequencing analysis. Needle biospies from 5 patients with other liver disorders were included as controls.
GSM2809205.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Alagille syndrome_liver needle biopsy' with titles 'Liver_ALGS1'..'Liver_ALGS5' is Alagille cholestatic liver disease.

GSM2809206.title: Liver_ALGS5
GSM2809206.study_title: RNA Seq of Alagille liver biopsies
GSM2809206.study_summary: Needle biopsies were performed to obtain liver samples from patients for clinical purposes from patients with Alagille syndrome. A small portion was snap frozen and later used for RNA sequencing analysis. Needle biospies from 5 patients with other liver disorders were included as controls.
GSM2809206.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Alagille syndrome_liver needle biopsy' with titles 'Liver_ALGS1'..'Liver_ALGS5' is Alagille cholestatic liver disease.

GSM2817112.title: PHH 0d
GSM2817112.study_title: Gene expression patterns define the hepatocyte-like cells derived by different strategies [RNA-seq]
GSM2817112.study_summary: We performed genome-wide gene profiling in hiPSC-Hep and hiHep and compared gene expression patterns in these two types of hepatocyte-like cells.Genes involving in fat digestion and absorption and metabolism enzymes were induced in both hiHeps and hiPSC-Heps. There were also hepatic genes not expressed in either hiPSC-Heps or hiHeps, including cytochrome P450-based metabolism genes and coagulation
GSM2817112.llm_rationale: sample titles 'PHH 0d | PHH 1d | PHH 2d | PHH 4d' are Primary Human Hepatocytes cultured over a time course as reference.

GSM2453420.title: polyA mRNA RNA-seq from liver (ENCLB490UAX)
GSM2453420.summary: Homo sapiens liver tissue male adult (18 years)
GSM2453420.study_summary: RNA-seq of human liver tissue. The second technical replicate was performed in May 2015 and was not part of the original Lin et al. 2014 publication (PMID:25413365).  For data usage terms and conditions, please refer to http://www.genome.gov/27528022 and http://www.genome.gov/Pages/Research/ENCODE/ENCODE_Data_Use_Policy_for_External_Users_03-07-14.pdf
GSM2453420.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Homo sapiens liver tissue male adult (18 years)' from ENCODE with no disease indication.

GSM2547178.title: A8A_hep4_hHep
GSM2547178.study_title: Multilineage communication regulates human liver bud self-organization from pluripotency II
GSM2547178.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2547178.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_adult_liver' with titles like 'A12B_hep25_hHep' indicates primary adult human hepatocyte (hHep) references, not iPSC-derived cells.

GSM2547210.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2547210.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_adult_liver' with titles like 'A12B_hep25_hHep' indicates primary adult human hepatocyte (hHep) references, not iPSC-derived cells.

GSM2547252.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_adult_liver' with titles like 'A12B_hep25_hHep' indicates primary adult human hepatocyte (hHep) references, not iPSC-derived cells.

Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2547294.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_fetal_liver' and titles like 'A10A_fetalHep2_fetal17' indicate primary fetal hepatocyte references.

GSM2547335.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_fetal_liver' and titles like 'A10A_fetalHep2_fetal17' indicate primary fetal hepatocyte references.

GSM2547418.study_summary: Conventional 2-D differentiation from pluripotency fails to recapitulate cell interactions occurring during organogenesis. 3-D organoids generate complex organ-like tissues, however it is unclear how heterotypic interactions impact lineage identity. Here we use single-cell RNA-seq to reconstruct hepatocyte-like lineage progression from pluripotency in 2-D culture. We then derive 3-D liver bud (LB)
GSM2547418.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_fetal_liver' and titles like 'A10A_fetalHep2_fetal17' indicate primary fetal hepatocyte references.

GSM2547459.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human_fetal_liver' and titles like 'A10A_fetalHep2_fetal17' indicate primary fetal hepatocyte references.

GSM2563043.title: Human RNAseq Liver Female1 Adult
GSM2563043.study_title: BioProject PRJNA381064: Convergent origination of a Drosophila-like dosage compensation mechanism in a reptile lineage (Gene expression profiling in several tetrapod species, bulk tissue RNA-seq)
GSM2563043.study_summary: In order to study the green anole dosage compensation mechanism we generated strand-specific RNA-seq libraries for a total of 186 samples from the green anole and other representative amniotes (human, mouse, opossum, platypus, chicken and xenopus). Moreover, in order to understand the dosage compensation mechanism of A. carolinensis, we generated ChIP-Seq data for the histone modification H4K16ac
GSM2563043.llm_rationale: Sample titles 'Human RNAseq Liver Female1/Male1 Adult' from a cross-species dosage compensation study with no disease indication.

GSM2677879.title: 1334/E47 NT1
GSM2677879.summary: PDX (patient-derived) cell line from a liver metastasis from a primary pancreas tumor transduced with a retrovirus that expressed human E47
GSM2677879.study_title: RNA-sequencing profiles of five different pancreas cancer cell lines that were engineered to express a form of the basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor, E47, that localizes to the nucleus in response to tamoxifen.
GSM2677879.study_summary: E47 had been shown previously to inhibit cell cycle progression in pancreas cancer cell lines. This study was designed to identify global changes in gene expression that occurred in response to E47 with the goal of identifying molecular mechanisms involved inE47-mediated inhibition of the cell cycle.
GSM2677879.llm_rationale: Sample summary states 'PDX cell line from a liver metastasis from a primary pancreas tumor', making it a non-liver primary metastasis that grew in liver.

GSM2701558.study_title: RNA sequencing to compare gene expession in hepatic stellate cells on a soft (400 Pa) and stiff (25000 Pa) polyacrylamide hydrogels
GSM2701558.study_summary: Primary human hepatic stellate cells (HSCs) isolated from healthy patients were purchased from Sciencell.  They were seeded on either 400 Pa or 25000 Pa polyacrylamide hydrogels for 2 days and collected for RNA isolation.  The goal of this study is to determine genes transcriptionally regulated by a stiff matrix. 2 RNA samples of HSCs on 400 Pa and 3 RNA samples of HSCs on 25600 Pa were sent to th
GSM2701558.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Liver hepatic stellate cells' from a study comparing 'gene expression in hepatic stellate cells on hydrogels' confirms primary HSCs.

GSM2459128.title: 1_ctrl_shRNA_vehicle
GSM2459128.study_title: Synectin Promotes Fibrogenesis by Regulating PDGFR Isoforms Through Distinct Mechanisms
GSM2459128.study_summary: The scaffold protein synectin plays a critical role in the trafficking and regulation of membrane receptor pathways.   As the platelet derived growth factor receptor (PDGFR)  pathway is essential for hepatic stellate cell (HSC) activation and liver fibrosis, we sought to determine the role of synectin on the PDGFR pathway in HSC.  To study the role of synectin in the development of liver fibrosis,
GSM2459128.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'liver' with shRNA/PDGF-treated titles matches the study's primary human hepatic stellate cells.

GSM2330093.title: T34-CXCR6p
GSM2330093.study_title: Comparison of Eomes-negative and Eomes-positive human liver NK cells by RNASeq
GSM2330093.study_summary: We sorted Eomes-negative NK cells (CD3- CD56+ CXCR6- CD16-) and Eomes-positive NK cells (CD3- CD56+ CXCR6+) from total leukocytes isolated from the perfusion fluid of five healthy human livers destined for transplantation. Total RNA was extracted from sorted cells, cDNA generated and RNASeq performed.
GSM2330093.llm_rationale: Sample summary 'Healthy liver leukocytes' and study describes 'NK cells sorted from perfusion fluid of healthy human livers'.

GSM2343909.title: small RNA-seq from right lobe of liver (ENCLB496AQV)
GSM2343909.summary: Homo sapiens right lobe of liver tissue female adult (53 years)
GSM2343909.study_title: small RNA-seq from right lobe of liver (ENCSR603XUX)
GSM2343909.study_summary: The libraries contained in this Experiment come from tissue sub-sections of a 53 year old female's right lobe of liver tissue section obtained from GTEx. They are stranded PE101 Illumina Hi-Seq RNA-Seq libraries from rRNA-depleted Total RNA < 200 nucleotides in size.  For data usage terms and conditions, please refer to http://www.genome.gov/27528022 and http://www.genome.gov/Pages/Research/ENCODE
GSM2343909.llm_rationale: ENCODE 'right lobe of liver tissue female adult (53 years)' small RNA-seq, healthy liver.

GSM2836481.title: CD34_chromRNA
GSM2836481.study_title: Targeted enhancer activation by a subunit of the integrator complex
GSM2836481.study_summary: We investigated the genomic occupancy of INTS13 during monocytic differentiation, in in vitro differentiated monocytes and a leukemia-derived cell line. We produced genome wide data for steady state RNA (total RNA-seq) and chromatin-associated RNA (chromatin RNA-seq). Further, we used ChIP-seq to examine genome-wide distribution of INTS13, INTS11, EGR1 and NAB2, as well as the changes in occupancy
GSM2836481.llm_rationale: Sample is 'fetal liver derived CD34+ cells' (CD34_chromRNA), hematopoietic progenitors not liver parenchyma.

GSM2480437.study_title: RNA sequencing (RNA-SEQ) of 21 HBV-HCC patients with non-neoplastic liver and tumor tissues
GSM2480437.study_summary: Purpose: Chronic Hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection leads to liver fibrosis which is a major risk factor in Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) and an independent risk factor of recurrence after HCC tumor resection. HBV genome can be inserted into human genome, and chronic inflammation may trigger somatic mutations. Several studies characterized HBV integration sites in HCC patients with regard to frequ
GSM2480437.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Non-neoplastic liver tissue' with titles 'Non-neoplastic liver tissue of P105' etc. is matched non-tumor from HBV-HCC patients.

GSM2480451.study_summary: Purpose: Chronic Hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection leads to liver fibrosis which is a major risk factor in Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) and an independent risk factor of recurrence after HCC tumor resection. HBV genome can be inserted into human genome, and chronic inflammation may trigger somatic mutations. Several studies characterized HBV integration sites in HCC patients with regard to frequ
GSM2480451.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Non-neoplastic liver tissue' with titles 'Non-neoplastic liver tissue of P105' etc. is matched non-tumor from HBV-HCC patients.

GSM2715277.title: LIVER1
GSM2715277.study_title: Whole transcriptome analysis of iPSC-derived hepatic organoids cultures
GSM2715277.study_summary: A heatmap, which focused upon 2458 genes that were involved in stem cell differentiation and liver development, was prepared to visualize the relative relationship between the transcriptomes of iPSCs, PHHs, human liver tissue and HO1. GSM2715277.llm_rationale: Sample summary states 'normal liver tissue' with titles LIVER1/LIVER2, used as control for an organoid study.

GSM1849373.title: MIHA EV
GSM1849373.study_title: Differential gene expression profiling of MIHA cells transfected with C-terminal truncated HBx mutants
GSM1849373.study_summary: To compare the differentially expressed transcriptomes between MIHA cells transfected with empty vector control or different C-terminal truncated HBx mutants (14 or 35 amino acid carboxyl-terminal truncation - i.e. d14 and d35)
GSM1849373.llm_rationale: Sample title 'MIHA EV' is the immortalized human liver MIHA cell line with empty vector.

GSM2653819.title: Healthy1-Tissue_1 [RNA-Seq]
GSM2653819.study_title: Patient-derived organoid cultures model human primary liver cancer in vitro
GSM2653819.study_summary: The study of human liver cancer has been hampered by the lack of reliable models that faithfully recapitulate the physiopathology of the patient’s original tumour ex vivo. Here, we describe the first culture system to allow the establishment and long-term expansion of human primary liver cancer (PLC) organoids (called tumoroids) from the three most common PLC subtypes: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HC
GSM2653819.llm_rationale: sample_summary 'Human liver tissue' with titles 'CC1-Tissue', 'CHC1-Tissue' etc. are primary liver cancer tissue (mixed CC/HCC/CHC), defaulting to HCC as the dominant hepatocellular signal.
